tool.name: Topaz Preprocess
tool.description: Downsample and normalize micrographs in one step
tool.command:
#import re mkdir -p input_images && mkdir -p output_images && #for $img in $input_images: #set $clean_name = re.sub('[^\w\-\.]', '_', str($img.element_identifier)) ln -s '$img' 'input_images/$clean_name' && #end for
topaz preprocess -s $scale $affine $metadata --sample $sample --niters $niters -a $alpha -b $beta --device $device --num-workers \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1} --num-threads $num_threads --format '$format_' -o output_images input_images/*

param.name: scale
param.label: Downsampling Factor
param.help: Downsample images by this factor. Default: 1.

param.name: affine
param.label: Use Standard Affine Normalization
param.help: If checked, uses (x - mean)/std of the whole image instead of the 2-component Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM).

param.name: sample
param.label: Pixel Sampling Factor
param.help: Pixel sampling factor for model fit. Speeds up estimation but introduces sample error if > 1.

param.name: niters
param.label: Maximum EM Iterations
param.help: Maximum number of EM iterations to run for GMM model fit.

param.name: alpha
param.label: Alpha parameter
param.help: Alpha parameter of the beta distribution prior on the GMM mixing proportion.

param.name: beta
param.label: Beta parameter
param.help: Beta parameter of the beta distribution prior on the GMM mixing proportion.

param.name: metadata
param.label: Save parameter metadata
param.help: Save parameter metadata for each micrograph in JSON format.

tool.help:
**What it does**
This tool downsamples and normalizes images (micrographs) using Topaz's preprocess subcommand. It utilizes a 2-component Gaussian mixture model (GMM) by default to handle background and carbon pixels, standardizing exposures across micrographs for more accurate downstream training and particle picking.
